source_jobs.title: Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Homera Health


Homera Health has built some of today’s largest and fastest-growing D2C telemedicine brands. As we expand existing and new market operations, we’re hiring world-class talent across product design, front-end and back-end engineering, digital marketing, and operations to join us in building the next generation of virtual healthcare experiences.



About the Role

Homera Health is looking for a hands-on Marketing Manager to help plan and execute our marketing efforts as we grow. This role is ideal for someone who is comfortable working independently, enjoys owning work end to end, and can move between planning and execution without a lot of structure or oversight.

You'll work closely with leadership and external partners to run marketing initiatives across lifecycle marketing, paid media creative production, educational content, social media, and website/landing page projects. This role requires both strong execution skills and the ability to think strategically about how channels work together.



Responsibilities:

Marketing Planning & Coordination

 - Plan and execute cross-channel marketing initiatives in alignment with evolving priorities

 - Own marketing calendars, timelines, and documentation to ensure visibility and accountability

 - Coordinate work with external vendors, freelancers, and partners

 - Manage campaign launches, updates, and executional follow-through

 - Support day-to-day marketing operations and project management across initiatives

Lifecycle Marketing (LCM)

 - Plan and execute lifecycle campaigns across email and SMS, supporting key stages of the customer journey from onboarding through engagement and retention

 - Build, manage, and update core lifecycle flows

 - Partner with leadership to align lifecycle initiatives with business priorities

Paid Media – Creative Production

 - Plan and manage creative production for paid channels (Meta, Google, etc.)

 - Develop creative briefs, messaging direction, and testing approaches

 - Coordinate asset creation, feedback, and iteration with designers and external partners

 - Ensure paid creative and messaging are aligned with landing page, onboarding, and lifecycle experiences

Website & Landing Page Projects

 - Coordinate and manage website updates and landing page projects

 - Work with designers, developers, and stakeholders to ensure projects are delivered on time

 - Ensure landing pages align with campaign goals, messaging, and conversion objectives

 - Support ongoing optimization and testing efforts to improve performance

Educational Content Production

 - Plan, produce, and distribute educational content across marketing channels

 - Ensure educational content supports conversion, onboarding, and retention goals

Social Media Management

 - Own social media planning and execution across priority platforms

 - Develop content calendars and oversee posting and engagement

 - Ensure social content aligns with brand, education, and growth objectives
   
   

Qualifications

 - 3–7 years of hands-on marketing experience in early-stage or fast-moving environments, with direct ownership of campaign execution

 - Experience managing one or more of the following areas: content production, social media, paid social, lifecycle marketing (LCM), conversion rate optimization (CRO), or related marketing functions

 - Exposure to performance marketing creative testing and iteration

 - Demonstrated ability to plan and run campaigns end to end across multiple channels without heavy oversight

 - Practical experience coordinating creative, content, lifecycle, and/or web projects simultaneously

 - Comfortable using performance results to prioritize work and make improvements

 - Proven ability to stay organized, manage competing priorities, and deliver consistently in ambiguous environments

 - Clear communicator who can work effectively with leadership and external partners
   
   

Nice to Have

 - Experience in health, wellness, or reg

source_jobs.title: Patient Services Representative
source_jobs.description:
This is a fully remote role.

 

About Homera Health

Homera Health has built some of today’s largest and fastest-growing D2C telemedicine brands. As we expand existing and new market operations, we’re hiring world-class talent across product design, front-end and back-end engineering, digital marketing, and operations to join us in building the next generation of virtual healthcare experiences.



About the role:

We are looking for a Patient Services Representative who is empathetic, detail-oriented, process-driven, and comfortable working in a fast-paced, highly regulated healthcare support environment. This role sits at the center of the patient experience and requires strong written communication, ownership, and accountability across multiple workflows and tools.



Responsibilities:

 - Serve as a facilitator between the patient and the company, ensuring a seamless experience.

 - Answering questions about the company, offering accurate information about our products and services

 - Delivering information about the product.

 - Ensuring patient satisfaction and maintaining professional patient support.

 - Handling patient complaints and identifying the appropriate response and strategy to solve customers' issues as quickly as possible.

 - Keep records of patient interactions, process patient accounts, and file documents.

 - Take the extra mile to engage with patients.

 - Stay updated with products, policies, and services to provide informed support.
   
   

Requirements:

 - 1-3+ years of experience in customer support, patient support, or a related service-driven role

 - Experience supporting customers or patients via written channels such as chat, email, or ticketing systems

 - Prior experience in healthcare, telehealth, pharmacy, insurance, medical offices, or other HIPAA-regulated environments is required

 - Phone-based customer or patient support experience is a strong plus, especially in healthcare or service environments

 

Nice to have:

 - Experience working in telemedicine or related medical contexts.

 - Experience supporting customers through the Intercom platform.



What’s in it for you?

As a full-time member of our team, you’ll enjoy:

 - Flexible working hours

 - Work wherever you choose.

 - Fun and casual work environment.

 - We are a diverse, global team!

source_jobs.title: Solutions Architect II
source_jobs.description:
About Homera Health


Homera Health has built some of today’s largest and fastest-growing D2C telemedicine brands. As we expand existing and new market operations, we’re hiring world-class talent across product design, front-end and back-end engineering, digital marketing, and operations to join us in building the next generation of virtual healthcare experiences.

About the Role

As a Solution Architect II, you'll be empowered to define your own work and set the technical vision for assigned projects. Given broad business direction, you'll generate new frameworks, concepts, and methodologies within the solution architecture field. You'll be responsible for crafting your own roadmap and creating challenging yet achievable projects, fostering an environment of continuous learning and growth.




Responsibilities

 - Strategic Collaboration: Collaborate with stakeholders across various departments to understand their needs and challenges at a strategic level.

 - Complex Requirements Analysis: Analyze complex business requirements and translate them into innovative and future-proof technical solutions.

 - Concept Generation: Generate and promote new concepts, principles, and methods within the solution architecture discipline.

 - Solution Design and Architecture: Design and architect highly scalable, secure, and cost-effective solutions, considering emerging technologies and industry best practices.

 - Technology Integration: Select and integrate cutting-edge technologies (hardware, software, cloud) to build robust and efficient solutions.

 - Technical Documentation: Develop and maintain comprehensive technical documentation to ensure knowledge transfer and ongoing solution effectiveness.

 - Presentation Leadership: Lead presentations and discussions, effectively defending your proposed solutions to both technical and non-technical audiences.

 - Implementation Collaboration: Collaborate with development teams to ensure seamless implementation of your designed solutions.

 - Optimization Identification: Identify opportunities for optimization within existing systems and propose innovative solutions to improve overall efficiency.

 - Emerging Technologies Monitoring: Stay at the forefront of emerging technologies, actively evaluating and integrating them into your solution strategy.

 - Mentorship and Knowledge Sharing: Mentor junior Solution Architects and share your expertise by contributing to internal knowledge sharing initiatives.
   
   
   

Qualifications

 - Bachelor's degree in computer science, IT, or a related field (Master's degree a plus)

 - Minimum 7 years of experience in solution architecture or a related technical role (e.g., software development, DevOps, systems engineering)

 - 3-5 years of experience in solution design and implementation, with expertise in a specific domain (e.g., cloud computing, security)

 - Proven track record of successfully leading and executing complex technical projects

 - Mastery of a broad range of technical skills relevant to the role, including cloud computing, enterprise architecture, and IT security

 - Strong ability to generate innovative solutions and develop new approaches within the solution architecture field

 - Strong communicator who can bridge the gap between technical and non-technical audiences

 - Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to think strategically and consider long-term implications

 - Certification in solution architecture (e.g., AWS/Azure Solutions Architect or Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect)

 - Experience with enterprise integration patterns and best practices

 - Hands-on experience with cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, Google Cloud)

 - Strong understanding of microservices architecture and containerization technologies (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes)

 - Ability to create and maintain comprehensive documentation of architecture designs and solutions

 - Experience with automation and orchestrat

notes: Telemedicine company with a mix of marketing, operations, and tech roles.
